rulings.Case Title: ANDREW POSSELT, M.D., PH.D VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: SPECIALLY SET HEARING ON EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER FOR ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY CALENDAR for Friday, Mar-27-2026. LINE 12. SPECIALLY SET HEARING ON EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER FOR ALTERNATIVE WRIT OF MANDATE AND TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER. (TO BE HEARD BY JUDGE VAN AKEN AT 1:30 PM) Hearing required. For this specially-set 1:30 p.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Plaintiffs Motion To Amend Judgment To Add T24 Llc, Legal Recovery Llc, And Legal Recover Llc As Judgment Debtors
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY CALENDAR for Friday, Mar-27-2026. LINE 2. THIRD PARTIES T24 LLC, LEGAL RECOVERY LLC, LEGAL RECOVER LLC, J1K5 LLC, and VI TRAN'S Motion To Set Aside Order Granting Plaintiffs Motion To Amend Judgment To Add T24 Llc, Legal Recovery Llc, And Legal Recover Llc As Judgment Debtors. Defendants' (T24 LLC, Legal Recovery LLC and Legal Recover LLC) motion to set aside the 5/28/25 order adding them as judgment debtors is DENIED. On 2/25/26, defendants moved to vacate the 5/28/25 order and raise the same arguments. By order dated 2/25/26, this court denied the motion because, inter alia, movants argued that the order was void on its face but relied on the Leeds Diston declaration for relief. There is no basis to revisit this court's 2/26/25 ruling, which addressed the merits, because movants do not demonstrate a basis for reconsideration of that order or renewal of this motion to vacate under CCP 1008. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/REQ)
